page: Amazon Web Services r6a.16xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 64 vCPUs, 32 physical cores, and a 3.6 GHz clock speed. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, yielding a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Benchmark data shows strong performance in several key areas. The instance achieves top 25% results in stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests, as well as Passmark CPU and memory scores. Database operations and Redis SET operations are highly efficient, with Redis reaching 68.81 million operations per second. Memory bandwidth for cached small blocks is also in the top 25%, though uncached large-block read and write bandwidth falls into the average category. Software compilation, ray tracing, and image processing benchmarks also demonstrate strong performance.

The r6a.16xlarge offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive workloads due to its high memory density and dedicated CPU allocation. However, tradeoffs include the lack of local NVMe storage, which requires network storage dependencies, and the use of DDR4 memory rather than newer generations. Additionally, while small-model LLM inference is strong, medium and large-model inference speeds are average.

This instance is configured for memory-intensive applications such as large-scale databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, data processing pipelines, and enterprise applications requiring high RAM-to-vCPU ratios without the need for local physical SSDs or GPU acceleration.
description: Amazon Web Services r6a.16xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance featuring 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it delivers 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory, establishing an 8 GB per core ratio. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, but provides a 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks indicate strong capabilities in multi-core CPU execution, database operations, and Redis SET operations, which exceed 68.81 million operations per second. Conversely, uncached large-block memory bandwidth and medium-model LLM inference speeds remain average. This hardware profile is engineered for memory-heavy workloads, including in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise data processing applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6a.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 64 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR4 RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7R13 CPU, delivering strong database and multi-core performance without local storage.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r6a.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR4 RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86 server featuring high RAM density and strong multi-core performance for database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 512 GB DDR4 memory providing an 8:1 RAM-to-vCPU ratio
AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with a 3.6 GHz clock speed
Strong multi-core CPU, database, and Redis SET performance
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for in-memory databases, caching, and data processing
